title: Social infrastructure and centralisation in community response and recovery from disasters
content: Disaster response and recovery are most effective when community-led networks combine central coordination with reciprocal collaboration, highlighting the critical role of local social infrastructure in resilience.
snippet: Disaster response and recovery are most effective when community-led networks combine central coordination with reciprocal collaboration, highlighting the critical role of local social infrastructure

title: Responding to droughts: learning from crises
content: The report highlights how worsening droughts driven by climate change are exposing major gaps between humanitarian policy and practice, emphasizing the urgent need for more effective, locally grounded, and timely responses.
snippet: The report highlights how worsening droughts driven by climate change are exposing major gaps between humanitarian policy and practice, emphasizing the urgent need for more effective, locally grounded

title: How Pacific communities use sea worms to track time and seasonal shifts through a changing climate
content: The article explores how the annual palolo worm spawning in the Pacific sustains Indigenous ecological calendars, cultural traditions, and climate resilience.
snippet: The article explores how the annual palolo worm spawning in the Pacific sustains Indigenous ecological calendars, cultural traditions, and climate resilience.

title: Marine heatwaves ‘nearly double’ the economic damage caused by tropical cyclones
content: Tropical cyclones that rapidly intensify when passing over marine heatwaves can become “supercharged”, increasing the likelihood of high economic losses, a new study finds.
snippet: Tropical cyclones that rapidly intensify when passing over marine heatwaves can become “supercharged”, increasing the likelihood of high economic losses, a new study finds.

title: Fixing Baltimore’s unequal weather data coverage
content: A new partnership between researchers and community members created a comprehensive network of weather stations across underserved areas of the city.
snippet: A new partnership between researchers and community members created a comprehensive network of weather stations across underserved areas of the city.

title: Vulnerability Insight and Action (VIA)
content: Vulnerability Insight and Action's mission is to reduce human and animal suffering and vulnerability to disasters and crises.
snippet: Vulnerability Insight and Action's mission is to reduce human and animal suffering and vulnerability to disasters and crises.

title: Massive Australian bushfire impacts: it's our fault
content: This report examines how large, severe bushfires in South East Australia are causing widespread environmental, social, and economic damage, worsened by inadequate fire management and increasing fire intensity.
snippet: This report examines how large, severe bushfires in South East Australia are causing widespread environmental, social, and economic damage, worsened by inadequate fire management and increasing fire i

title: What Türkiye’s AFAD-DEHAS platform means for earthquake risk reduction
content: Türkiye has developed a new system: the AFAD Earthquake Early Loss and Damage Analysis System (AFAD-DEHAS). AFAD-DEHAS is a web-based platform that allows multiple users to run analyses simultaneously.
snippet: Türkiye has developed a new system: the AFAD Earthquake Early Loss and Damage Analysis System (AFAD-DEHAS). AFAD-DEHAS is a web-based platform that allows multiple users to run analyses simultaneously

title: Nature-based coastal flood protection: Lessons from the Caribbean and the Philippines
content: This short communication examines four case studies from the Caribbean and the Philippines to summarize practical lessons on the implementation of nature-based solutions (NbS) for coastal flood protection
snippet: This short communication examines four case studies from the Caribbean and the Philippines to summarize practical lessons on the implementation of nature-based solutions (NbS) for coastal flood protec

title: Pacific regional framework on climate mobility implementation plan 2025-2030
content: The Pacific Regional Framework on Climate Mobility (PRFCM) serves as a guide for Pacific Islands Forum (Forum) governments, communities, non-state actors and partners in ensuring rights-based and people-centred movement in the context of climate change.
snippet: The Pacific Regional Framework on Climate Mobility (PRFCM) serves as a guide for Pacific Islands Forum (Forum) governments, communities, non-state actors and partners in ensuring rights-based and peop

title: The Pacific Island Forum
content: The Pacific Islands Forum brings the region together to address pressing issues and challenges, and foster collaboration and cooperation in the pursuit of shared goals.
snippet: The Pacific Islands Forum brings the region together to address pressing issues and challenges, and foster collaboration and cooperation in the pursuit of shared goals.

title: After the storm, what remains is hope
content: Typhoon Kalmaegi caused significant damage to housing, livelihoods, and public services in Gia Lai province. However, what stands out is the strong sense of solidarity within communities.
snippet: Typhoon Kalmaegi caused significant damage to housing, livelihoods, and public services in Gia Lai province. However, what stands out is the strong sense of solidarity within communities.

title: Maasai community governance of groundwater access during drought in Northern Tanzania
content: This brief documents a groundwater access system maintained by Maasai pastoral communities in northern Tanzania, where distributed water access points are governed through community leadership structures and customary institutions.
snippet: This brief documents a groundwater access system maintained by Maasai pastoral communities in northern Tanzania, where distributed water access points are governed through community leadership structu

title: Call for nature‑based solutions as climate change deepens the wildfire threat
content: Forest fires have risen sharply over the past decade. In the first half of 2024 alone, more than 5,000 incidents were recorded across 74 districts, with fires becoming more frequent, intense and difficult to control.
snippet: Forest fires have risen sharply over the past decade. In the first half of 2024 alone, more than 5,000 incidents were recorded across 74 districts, with fires becoming more frequent, intense and diffi

title: Comoros forest restoration offers a lesson in climate resilience
content: Communities played a central role in restoring degraded land by replanting trees in forests areas and adopting climate-smart farming methods. These techniques helped farmers produce more from existing plots, putting less pressure on forests.
snippet: Communities played a central role in restoring degraded land by replanting trees in forests areas and adopting climate-smart farming methods. These techniques helped farmers produce more from existing

title: Pakistan launches ‘first-ever’ water, sanitation database amid mounting climate risks
content: Pakistan cannot achieve climate resilience without reliable information on who has access to clean water and sanitation, where the gaps are and how public resources are being used.
snippet: Pakistan cannot achieve climate resilience without reliable information on who has access to clean water and sanitation, where the gaps are and how public resources are being used.

title: A lasting lesson and teaching event legacy of the 1961 Western Australian bushfires
content: Prescribed burning emerged as a critical component of the Western Australia’s fire strategy. Research showed that under severe weather, fuel older than five years in jarrah forests and seven years in karri forests could carry uncontrollable crown fires.
snippet: Prescribed burning emerged as a critical component of the Western Australia’s fire strategy. Research showed that under severe weather, fuel older than five years in jarrah forests and seven years in

title: U of A research finds improved weather forecasts could reduce heat deaths as climate warms
content: Researchers found that improving short-term temperature forecasts in alignment with expert predictions of technological development could reduce U.S. mortality from heat by 18% to 25% in the year 2100.
snippet: Researchers found that improving short-term temperature forecasts in alignment with expert predictions of technological development could reduce U.S. mortality from heat by 18% to 25% in the year 2100

title: AI and drones team up to find climate-resilient wheat
content: AI and drones are being used to identify climate-resilient wheat varieties that balance high yields with climate stability.
snippet: AI and drones are being used to identify climate-resilient wheat varieties that balance high yields with climate stability.

title: Method offers a more realistic way to judge how near-fault structures perform in earthquakes
content: A magnitude-based method (MIDA) improves the accuracy and realism of assessing how critical infrastructure performs during near-fault earthquakes by using physics-based simulations instead of scaled historical data.

title: GIR 2025 working paper: Irrigation
content: This working paper highlights irrigation infrastructure as a critical lever for global food security.
snippet: This working paper highlights irrigation infrastructure as a critical lever for global food security.

title: Post disaster analysis report for Elgeyo Marakwet landslides
content: This study presents a participatory post-disaster analysis of recurrent landslides in Elgeyo Marakwet County, Kenya, focusing on the October 2025 event that caused significant loss of life, displacement, and livelihood disruption.
snippet: This study presents a participatory post-disaster analysis of recurrent landslides in Elgeyo Marakwet County, Kenya, focusing on the October 2025 event that caused significant loss of life, displaceme

title: Gender in climate and disaster risk finance and insurance in Bangladesh
content: This analysis attempts to map the current climate and disaster risk finance and insurance (CDRFI) policies and programmes, provide diagnoses of systemic gender gaps, and formulate evidence-informed recommendations in Bangladesh.
snippet: This analysis attempts to map the current climate and disaster risk finance and insurance (CDRFI) policies and programmes, provide diagnoses of systemic gender gaps, and formulate evidence-informed re

title: Gender analysis of climate and disaster risk finance and insurance in Rwanda
content: This publication constitutes a gender analysis of climate and disaster risk finance and insurance in Rwanda.
snippet: This publication constitutes a gender analysis of climate and disaster risk finance and insurance in Rwanda.

title: Zambia country climate and development report
content: This report outlines a set of reforms aimed at positioning Zambia on an upper-middle-income growth path by mid-century in the context of climate change.
snippet: This report outlines a set of reforms aimed at positioning Zambia on an upper-middle-income growth path by mid-century in the context of climate change.

title: Urban heat in Johannesburg and Ekurhuleni: impacts and mitigation options
content: Based on insights from the monitoring and modelling exercises conducted in Johannesburg and Ekurhuleni, concrete recommendations are formulated in this report to enhance the resilience of both metros to excessive heat.
snippet: Based on insights from the monitoring and modelling exercises conducted in Johannesburg and Ekurhuleni, concrete recommendations are formulated in this report to enhance the resilience of both metros

title: The Climate and Health Risk Index: Tracking climate change risk to health across national and subnational levels to prioritize adaptation investment
content: The purpose of the CHRI is to improve how climate change and health resources are invested.
snippet: The purpose of the CHRI is to improve how climate change and health resources are invested.

title: Shaken, but not deterred: Acute stressors and the formation of hope and aspirations among tertiary-educated youth in Myanmar in the aftermath of the 2025 earthquake
content: Using data from a sample of educated youth in Myanmar, this paper documents the relationship between various sources of chronic (ongoing and long-term) and acute (brief and intense) stressors with measures of hope and aspirations.
snippet: Using data from a sample of educated youth in Myanmar, this paper documents the relationship between various sources of chronic (ongoing and long-term) and acute (brief and intense) stressors with mea

title: Enhancing community flood adaptation by leveraging interdependencies between resilience capitals: A global empirical analysis
content: This study contributes to the development of integrated, systems-based adaptation strategies by examining how interactions among capitals shape resilience outcomes.
snippet: This study contributes to the development of integrated, systems-based adaptation strategies by examining how interactions among capitals shape resilience outcomes.

title: Artificial intelligence in wildland–urban interface wildfire management: a two‑phase review combining bibliometric mapping and thematic analysis
content: This study presents a two‑phase review of literature where artificial intelligence (AI) is applied in wildfire and WUI studies, combining bibliometric mapping for wildfires and a thematic content analysis for WUI.
snippet: This study presents a two‑phase review of literature where artificial intelligence (AI) is applied in wildfire and WUI studies, combining bibliometric mapping for wildfires and a thematic content anal

title: Nature-based solutions for climate adaptation: review of barriers to adoption and guidelines for policymakers
content: This paper supplements existing frameworks by providing practical recommendations for the whole decision-making cycle, from identification of objectives to the monitoring and scaling up of chosen interventions.
snippet: This paper supplements existing frameworks by providing practical recommendations for the whole decision-making cycle, from identification of objectives to the monitoring and scaling up of chosen inte

title: Towards an integrated rural fire management framework in Portugal
content: This policy paper brings together insights and recommendations from a two-year review of Portugal’s wildfire management reforms following the extreme fires of 2017.
snippet: This policy paper brings together insights and recommendations from a two-year review of Portugal’s wildfire management reforms following the extreme fires of 2017.

title: Mapping drought severity in Mexico using high‑resolution satellite data
content: This paper analyses drought severity across Mexican regions between 2000 and 2025 using satellite-based indicators of vegetation health and surface moisture.
snippet: This paper analyses drought severity across Mexican regions between 2000 and 2025 using satellite-based indicators of vegetation health and surface moisture.

title: What are the likely impacts of rising temperatures on students and how are countries adapting?
content: This policy brief summarises the results and discusses the policy implications of an assessment the number of schools and students at risk of rising temperatures in 2050 across 13 OECD countries and economies.
snippet: This policy brief summarises the results and discusses the policy implications of an assessment the number of schools and students at risk of rising temperatures in 2050 across 13 OECD countries and e

title: Using climate projections to assess increasing student exposure to high temperatures
content: This technical paper develops a methodology to assess the number of schools and students at risk of rising temperatures in 2050 across 13 OECD countries and economies.
snippet: This technical paper develops a methodology to assess the number of schools and students at risk of rising temperatures in 2050 across 13 OECD countries and economies.

title: Ecosystem-based disaster risk reduction: Implementing nature-based solutions for resilience
content: The purpose of this policy paper is to increase awareness of the important role of ecosystem-based approaches in reducing disaster risk.
snippet: The purpose of this policy paper is to increase awareness of the important role of ecosystem-based approaches in reducing disaster risk.

title: A catalogue of nature‑based solutions for coastal resilience
content: This Coastal Catalogue is designed to support the identification of investment opportunities and the integration of nature-based solutions (NBS) into coastal risk management and planning processes.
snippet: This Coastal Catalogue is designed to support the identification of investment opportunities and the integration of nature-based solutions (NBS) into coastal risk management and planning processes.

title: How does climate change affect educational outcomes? Key pathways
content: This brief outlines three key impact pathways: damage to school infrastructure, effects on children's health, and impact to household livelihoods.
snippet: This brief outlines three key impact pathways: damage to school infrastructure, effects on children's health, and impact to household livelihoods.

title: The impacts of climate change on education outcomes: assessment and response
content: This brief presents findings from a global systematic review examining how climate change affects primary and secondary education across low-, middle- and high-income countries.
snippet: This brief presents findings from a global systematic review examining how climate change affects primary and secondary education across low-, middle- and high-income countries.

title: Effects of early life exposure to climate shocks on education outcomes
content: This policy brief examines how exposure to climate shocks in early life has distinct and often persistent effects on educational outcomes and implications for life-time earnings.
snippet: This policy brief examines how exposure to climate shocks in early life has distinct and often persistent effects on educational outcomes and implications for life-time earnings.

title: Google Earth Engine and Machine Learning for Land Cover Mapping
content: This course introduces participants to the use of Google Earth Engine (GEE) and machine learning techniques for accurate and efficient land cover mapping.
snippet: This course introduces participants to the use of Google Earth Engine (GEE) and machine learning techniques for accurate and efficient land cover mapping.

title: 3rd Multi-Hazard Impact-Based Forecast and Warning System
content: This course builds professional capacity to design, operate, and strengthen people-centred, multi-hazard end-to-end early warning systems for hydro-meteorological and geological hazards under changing climate conditions.
